posts.hook: Developers average 11 hours of screen time. Your eyes weren't built for this.
posts.caption:
Developers average 11 hours of screen time per day.

Your eyes weren't designed for this.

Here's what happens to your ciliary muscle after 8 straight hours of near-focus:
- Accommodative spasm
- Blurred distance vision
- Chronic eye fatigue

The fix? 5 minutes of structured eye exercises.

#DevTok #Developer #ScreenTime #Axeo #EyeStrain #Programming

posts.hook: Software engineers have the highest rate of digital eye strain. Here's the data.
posts.caption:
Software engineers have the highest rate of digital eye strain in any profession.

Here's why — and what actually helps (thread):

1/ The average developer stares at a screen for 11+ hours. Your blink rate drops from 15/min to 4/min during focused coding.

2/ IDEs use small fonts. Dark mode helps contrast but doesn't fix the near-focus lock on your ciliary muscle.

3/ The 20-20-20 rule works but nobody remembers it. You need a system.

4/ We built Axeo for exactly this. 17 exercises based on clinical guidelines. 5 min/day in your browser.

Try free: axeo.vision/exercise
posts.visual_notes: Optional: infographic showing screen time by profession.
